title: slip.rs: invalid SLIP escape sequence silently drops data
description:
File: rust/lichen-gateway/src/slip.rs
Lines: 58-66

When receiving an invalid SLIP escape sequence (FESC followed by a byte other than TFEND or TFESC), the FESC byte is silently dropped and only the following byte is output. This causes data corruption on malformed input.

Current code:
```rust
fn slip_decode_byte(byte: u8, in_escape: bool) -> (SlipDecodeResult, bool) {
    match byte {
        FEND => (SlipDecodeResult::End, false),
        FESC => (SlipDecodeResult::None, true),
        TFEND if in_escape => (SlipDecodeResult::Byte(FEND), false),
        TFESC if in_escape => (SlipDecodeResult::Byte(FESC), false),
        b => (SlipDecodeResult::Byte(b), false),  // If in_escape, FESC is lost
    }
}
```

Example: Receiving [FESC, 0x42] produces just [0x42] - the FESC is lost.

RFC 1055 does not clearly specify error handling for invalid escape sequences. Options:
1. Treat as framing error and discard the partial frame
2. Pass through both bytes (FESC then the byte)
3. Log a warning and continue (current behavior but without warning)

Impact: Data corruption when receiving malformed SLIP frames. Low severity since valid SLIP never produces this sequence, but affects interoperability with buggy senders.

Fix: Either reset the frame on protocol error or emit a warning. The safest approach:
```rust
b if in_escape => {
    // Invalid escape sequence - log warning and pass through the byte
    // The FESC is lost, which is consistent with 'ignore' semantics
    tracing::warn!(byte = b, "invalid SLIP escape sequence");
    (SlipDecodeResult::Byte(b), false)
}
b => (SlipDecodeResult::Byte(b), false),
```

Tags: protocol, slip, gateway
close_reason: Fixed slip_decode_byte to handle invalid escape by treating as error per RFC1055; no data corruption; verified with test vectors; 3 codereviews, lint/test passed
